name: Yuki
message: "Perfect. I'm just as late as I planned."
I looked at my watch.
The time was exactly noon.
I was laughably late for school.

name: Yuki
message: "Jeez, being this late is downright beautiful."
I wondered if the Wakatsuki sisters would be angry again.
It was only yesterday that I skipped all my classes.
Well, if I'm going to be late anyway...
I might as well use my time wisely.
I took some papers that I'd printed out of my bag.
I discovered the Web Bot Project site last night.
The New White Lotus Association Web Bot Research Facility.

name: Yuki
message: "The location on that strange page..."
I looked at the map of the train stops.

name: Yuki
message: "This is an express train, so..."
That occult-looking research facility should be right around the third stop.

name: Yuki
message: "The New White Lotus Association Web Bot Research Facility..."
Incidentally, I'd heard about an emerging religion called the White Lotus Association.
It was pretty famous. Supposedly, it even had some political influence.
But this organization was the "New" White Lotus Association.
I wasn't sure if I should consider it a completely separate entity, or if I should think of it as a related organization.
Well, either way, it's probably some sort of religious organization... Or a new science fad... Like, mad scientists and stuff...

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe I should go check it out?"

name: Yuki
message: "I'm going to be late either way. And it can't be that dangerous if I just go and see what kind of place it is."
The origin of the prediction was so close to me, just three train stops away. The prophecy was made that close to where I live.
And it was causing all this ruckus.
There was no way I wouldn't be interested.

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe I should check it out."
I sat and waited until I got to the third station, where I got off.
The station was in a quiet residential area. It was totally unremarkable.
It was no different from the place where I live, but that wasn't too surprising.
Looking at the map I'd printed out, I headed to the place it indicated.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, this is the third block, so..."
Tokyo never changes.
Everywhere is pretty much the same.
And because of that, it was a bit of a pain locating my objective.

name: Yuki
message: "I can't find it."
Strange. I didn't see anything that looked like a research facility.
If I had to describe the place...
There was nothing but run-down apartments.

name: Yuki
message: "White Lotus Apartments, huh? Whoa, it's the same name."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
There was almost no one living there.

name: Yuki
message: "Does that mean there's only one occupied apartment?"
The post box only had one name on it. The rest of the slots were blank.
I checked the electricity and gas meters.
With the exception of one room, all of them had notes to stop service.

name: Yuki
message: "There really aren't many tenants here."
What's going on here?
This was all super suspicious.

name: Yuki
message: "But still..."
The only nameplate that wasn't blank simply said "Kotomi," with no last name listed.

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe it's the owner of the building. I wonder if she's living alone."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm, this is..."
Ridiculously suspicious, right?
It could be some rich guy's hobby...
Though that would describe pretty much every mad scientist.

name: Yuki
message: "Though that's probably not it at all. It's probably some old lady that got caught up with a cult."
The name "White Lotus" brought to mind that other religious organization. There were several branches of it around here.
And even if it hadn't, "White Lotus" does sound religious. Most people wouldn't name an apartment building that.
"White Lotus" is just like the lotus in the Lotus Sutra.
I believe the original title of the Lotus Sutra meant "The Sutra of the White Lotus of True Instruction."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm."

name: Yuki
message: "I walked onto the property without even thinking."
But still...

name: Yuki
message: "It's not all that old..."
The building didn't look very old, but it wasn't very well maintained.
If the owner is still living here, why is it so beaten up?

name: Yuki
message: "Though it's only my assumption that the owner is still living here."
My eyes met those of the girl who'd walked out the door.
The girl looked at me, shocked.
Of course she was shocked. No one else was supposed to live here, and yet I was loitering in the area.
"Wh-Why are you here?"

name: Yuki
message: "S-Sorry... I-I'm not a burglar or anything."
Oh, crap. Saying that made it sound like I actually was one.
"Wh-Who are you right now?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
"Who are you?"

name: Yuki
message: "Um, my name is Minakami, you see..."
"Yuki... san?"
"Yuki-san... I..."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Why do you know my name?"
"Oh!"
"E-Excuse me... Um, why are you here?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, nothing... Um..."
Crap. She cut right to the chase.
And she answered my question with another question.
I wasn't sure what to say given the situation.
"Do you want to come in, Yuki-san?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
"Do you want to come in... to the house?"
The house?
Isn't that kind of dangerous?
She looked younger than me.
I didn't think she could do me any harm.
But I didn't know if there was anyone else inside the house. That definitely would have been dangerous.
The building was completely normal, at least on the outside. What I could see of the entranceway looked normal, too.
It didn't look like there was any mysterious organization in there.
Though, of course, that didn't guarantee my safety.
I was still interested, though.
Yeah...
There was no way I could just go home after coming this far.

name: Yuki
message: "Well, I guess, if I wouldn't be a bother."
"You won't be."
Naturally...
The inside was normal too.
"What would you like to drink?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, don't worry about me."
"Does Oolong tea sound fine?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, sure."

name: Yuki
message: "But before that, could I ask why you know my name?"
"Sorry. You're right. That was a little creepy, wasn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, no, that's not what I mean."
"My name is

name: Hasaki
message: Mamiya Hasaki."

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya Hasaki... Huh? Mamiya?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. He's in your class."

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya Takuji..."

name: Hasaki
message: "Takuji is my father."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
The truth made me doubt what I was hearing.
Did she just say... father?
Takuji had... a daughter of this age?
Wh-What the...
So he married incredibly young? No, even then, that made no sense. What's going on here?

name: Yuki
message: "That's..."

name: Hasaki
message: "It was a joke."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, riiight. Wait, a joke?!"

name: Hasaki
message: "Takuji is my husband."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?!"

name: Hasaki
message: "That was also a joke."

name: Yuki
message: "No... Enough with that, already."

name: Hasaki
message: "Excuse me."

name: Hasaki
message: "I just thought it would help break the ice."

name: Yuki
message: "No, well... You don't have to worry about me."

name: Hasaki
message: "Excuse me."

name: Hasaki
message: "The truth is... I'm Mamiya Takuji's little sister."

name: Yuki
message: "Little sister?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes."
Why is Mamiya's little sister in a place like this?
What's the meaning of this?

name: Hasaki
message: "Ah... Mother."

name: Yuki
message: "Mother?"
Hasaki disappeared into the next room.
I could see into the room through the crack in the door.
That must have been her mother.
That's what Hasaki said, anyway.
She did look like she was old enough to be a mother.
But her posture was rigid, and her eyes were staring into space.
It was like...

name: Hasaki
message: "Ah, excuse me. My mom just..."

name: Yuki
message: "Your mother, you said..."
She was just gazing in one direction, without moving.

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. You can probably tell by looking. Our mother is a bit..."

name: Yuki
message: "Is she sick?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes."

name: Hasaki
message: "More importantly, why are you here at this house, Yuki-san? Even Nii-san doesn't come here."

name: Yuki
message: "Ah, sorry. I came after I saw this."

name: Hasaki
message: "Huh?"
I showed her the pages I'd printed out.

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh... This is why."

name: Yuki
message: "The address on the site brought me to this building."

name: Hasaki
message: "I see. So you saw the New White Lotus Association website."

name: Yuki
message: "Um... So are you guys really involved with some religion?"

name: Hasaki
message: "That's right. I think that's what it was originally."

name: Yuki
message: "Originally?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Now it's just an apartment building owned by the Mamiya family."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh. Sounds like you guys are pretty wealthy if you own an entire apartment complex."

name: Hasaki
message: "No. It's just because our father is from one of the older families. Apparently, it's a famous family out in the countryside."

name: Yuki
message: "But if your family is wealthy..."

name: Hasaki
message: "Ahaha. Apparently, my parents eloped, so they're not all that rich."

name: Hasaki
message: "I think the title on this building is in the name of one of our relatives, too."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, the name outside says it's the New White Lotus Association..."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah. It was related to the White Lotus Association that you've probably heard about."

name: Yuki
message: "Was, in the past tense?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. Now it's just an apartment building. It has nothing do with any religion."

name: Yuki
message: "So what relation does it have with the White Lotus Association now?"

name: Hasaki
message: "None at all. Well, I suppose there's still a relation concerning my mother."

name: Yuki
message: "With your mother?"

name: Hasaki
message: "A long time ago, my mother was part of the White Lotus Association. She apparently rose to a pretty high rank, though she was eventually excommunicated."

name: Yuki
message: "I see..."

name: Hasaki
message: "After that, maybe it was revenge, or some kind of love-hate thing, but she made a new organization with a similar name."

name: Yuki
message: "And that's the New White Lotus Association?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. At first, it had several members who had left the White Lotus Association, but then her heart suffered a blow."

name: Yuki
message: "Her heart?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. Several of our relatives died in quick succession."

name: Yuki
message: "Her relatives?"

name: Hasaki
message: "My father died from cancer, and my brother died in an accident."

name: Yuki
message: "Brother?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, I had a twin brother."
A twin brother. So he would've been younger than Mamiya Takuji.

name: Hasaki
message: "The two of them died in succession, and my mother became mentally ill."

name: Yuki
message: "And she ended up like this, huh?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. That's right."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So, in reality, the New White Lotus Association doesn't exist."

name: Hasaki
message: "I suppose so."

name: Yuki
message: "But the site is still up."

name: Hasaki
message: "My mother is always staring at the wall like that, but occasionally she turns on the computer and starts fervently investigating."

name: Yuki
message: "What's she looking for?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Who knows? I suppose it means something to her, but I can't figure it out."

name: Yuki
message: "So your mother made the Web Bot Project?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, no, that was an acquaintance."

name: Yuki
message: "You'd have to be pretty knowledgeable to make something like that."

name: Hasaki
message: "He would probably call it a 'gag.'"

name: Yuki
message: "A gag? Who'd make something like that as a joke? He sounds like a pain in the ass."

name: Yuki
message: "So is it really making predictions?"

name: Hasaki
message: "He said it would cross into the future soon."

name: Yuki
message: "Cross into the future?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. As he says, it gathers samples based on the words on the Internet and posts them as predictions."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Wh-What do you mean?"

name: Hasaki
message: "When it was first started, it took a full day for something to be posted after it became a topic on the Internet. Now it's been improved, so it can post a few seconds after a topic comes up on the Internet."

name: Hasaki
message: "It went from twenty-four hours to just a few seconds. Supposedly, it will soon pass the present time and start posting future events."

name: Yuki
message: "Ahahaha..."
That guy was a bit strange. Was he a dumbass? Who'd make something like that as a joke?

name: Yuki
message: "In other words, it's a fake prediction system."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. It only updates after something happens, but it does it so quickly that it can look like it's making predictions."

name: Hasaki
message: "I don't know all the details, but he said it was just three millimeters from the future."
That guy was an idiot. No doubt about it.

name: Hasaki
message: "So I don't think there's anyone who actually believes it. If you check the site after something happens, you'll see that it's just making predictions that've already happened."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. You could just look at the site right after something big happened."

name: Hasaki
message: "It would just add it to its list of predictions."
What's up with that? What a nonsensical invention. Why would someone make that, even as a gag?

name: Hasaki
message: "We really should take it down, but the only time Mom looks alive is when she's looking at that site."

name: Hasaki
message: "I doubt anyone else looks at the site...
Though you saw it and came out here, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, well, I have a bit of interest in nonsense like that."

name: Hasaki
message: "Nonsense?"

name: Yuki
message: "Well, 'new science' and all that. I like to keep an eye on that stuff."

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, do you?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, I guess."

name: Yuki
message: "So how long has your mom had this site up?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Since about seven years ago."

name: Yuki
message: "Do you know about that prediction?"

name: Hasaki
message: "What prediction?"

name: Yuki
message: "On the site, there's a prediction about the end of the world."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah. July 20th of this year, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "At the very least, that one's not in hindsight."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. It's in four days."

name: Yuki
message: "Then that really is a prediction, though we don't know if it's actually going to happen."

name: Hasaki
message: "That site is based on that prediction."

name: Yuki
message: "Based on it?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes, that the world will end on July 20th, 2012."

name: Hasaki
message: "My mother put up the site in order to prove that prediction was correct."

name: Hasaki
message: "Though there's no way she could actually make something like that."

name: Yuki
message: "Based on that prediction? Who made the prediction?"

name: Hasaki
message: "I heard it was something the founder of the White Lotus Association said."

name: Yuki
message: "I-I see. So it has to do with the White Lotus Association."

name: Hasaki
message: "No. It's already been recanted."

name: Yuki
message: "Recanted?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. They announced that we had avoided that calamity through the power of the founder."

name: Yuki
message: "When?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Seven years ago."

name: Yuki
message: "Around the time the New White Lotus Association was made?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. From the association's perspective, my mother was probably just a nuisance."

name: Hasaki
message: "The White Lotus Association probably could've used legal means against her, but apparently she had them in an awkward situation, and they didn't want to cause a fuss."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So the association left her alone..."

name: Hasaki
message: "They're a huge organization. They just ignored her."

name: Yuki
message: "Sorry for taking up your time like this."

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, no. It's not a big deal."

name: Yuki
message: "Well then..."

name: Yuki
message: "Yes?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Ah... excuse me. It's nothing."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm?"

name: Hasaki
message: "If something's ever troubling you, please come here any time."

name: Yuki
message: "Sure thing."

name: Hasaki
message: "And if you meet my brother, tell him his little sister asked him to stop by once in a while. We're all waiting for him."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, sure..."

name: Yuki
message: "You haven't seen your brother lately?"

name: Hasaki
message: "No. It seems like I can't."

name: Hasaki
message: "That's probably part of the curse."

name: Yuki
message: "Curse?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, it's nothing."

name: Yuki
message: "Anyway, if I see him, I'll tell him what you said."

name: Yuki
message: "Look, I know it might not be my place to say this, but I don't think it's anything like that."

name: Hasaki
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "After all, Hasaki-chan, I can't imagine anyone who wouldn't want to have a cute little sister like you. So I'm sure he hasn't forgotten about you."

name: Hasaki
message: "Um, Yuki..."

name: Yuki
message: "Yes?"

name: Hasaki
message: "You have a childhood friend, right? Two of them, actually."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, yeah. The Wakatsuki sisters."

name: Hasaki
message: "Do you like them?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? What's this, all of a sudden?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Oh, nothing... Uh..."

name: Yuki
message: "Of course I like them. They've been my friends since we were little."

name: Hasaki
message: "Okay."

name: Hasaki
message: "Thank you."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. See you."

name: Hasaki
message: "Bye."
Why did she ask that?
There were a lot of mysterious things about her, like the way she chose her words.
But still, I gathered plenty of information.
First of all, those predictions were all baloney.
Especially the thing about the world ending on the 20th.
The person who made that prediction was the founder of the White Lotus Association.
And the founder himself withdrew the prediction.

name: Yuki
message: "Well, I suppose that was obvious, wasn't it?"
There's no way a prediction like that could be real.
After I left her house, I went to school.
Classes were already over, but I wanted to see what was happening at school.
I'm not sure why, but I felt like I was being overwhelmed by anxiety myself.
Classes were already over...
Even though it was immediately after school...
There really weren't very many people around at all.
It looked like there weren't any clubs going on either.

name: Yuki
message: "I wonder what's going on."
It really was deserted. I hardly passed anyone on my way here.
Was today a holiday?
Huh?

name: Yuki
message: "No one's here."
Even if classes were over, it was weird how there was no one around.
Did something happen?

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-Who are you? What are you doing to my desk?"
The girl stared at me.

name: "I'm Tachibana Kimika, from the class next door."
message: Kimika
"I'm Tachibana Kimika,

name: Kimika
message: from the class next door."

name: Yuki
message: "Tachibana... Kimika-san?"

name: Kimika
message: "I see. So you don't know who I am."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, it's just that I'm not very good at remembering people's names or faces."

name: Kimika
message: "Could I ask what your name is?"

name: Yuki
message: "Minakami Yuki. Why do you ask?"

name: Kimika
message: "Minakami-san, is it?
Well then, goodbye!"

name: Yuki
message: "Ah!"
She ran away. That girl was...

name: Yuki
message: "Gotcha!"

name: Kimika
message: "Haa, haa... I run track, and yet you're even faster than me?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Really? I thought you were running pretty slow."

name: Kimika
message: "Th-That's rude! I'm super fast!"

name: Yuki
message: "I'm pretty sure you're not."

name: Kimika
message: "Haa... So then, what do you want?"

name: Yuki
message: "No, that's my line. What were you doing to my desk?"

name: Kimika
message: "Inspection."

name: Yuki
message: "Inspection?"

name: Kimika
message: "Yes, let's say I was taking notes for social studies."

name: Yuki
message: "Like I'm gonna believe that crap."

name: Kimika
message: "Haa... You're an awfully bothersome person, aren't you?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
Bothersome?
She was snooping around my desk and somehow I was the bothersome one?

name: Kimika
message: "I get it. You're the reason the savior is still inadequate."

name: Yuki
message: "The savior?"

name: Kimika
message: "Yes, Mamiya Takuji-sama."

name: Yuki
message: "Wait! Are you one of his followers?! Does he have followers?!"

name: Kimika
message: "What do you mean, does he? That's rude. There are quite a few people who have come to kneel at the savior's feet."

name: Yuki
message: "D-Don't tell me that's why there aren't very many people around."

name: Kimika
message: "It seems like they're all talking about it in the staff room, too. It's time for everyone to go home pretty soon."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What do you plan on doing?!"

name: Kimika
message: "It has nothing to do with you."

name: Yuki
message: "Ah!"
I instantly dodged.
This woman... is crazy.

name: Yuki
message: "A kn-knife..."

name: Kimika
message: "If you get in my way, I won't hold back, even against you."
Sure, she had a knife, but she moved like an amateur. She wasn't a real threat.

name: Kimika
message: "Hehehe. So you at least know that you're strong."
She held something up.
I tried to block it, but...
I failed to do so.

name: Yuki
message: "Guh!"
A bright light struck my eyes. I was looking straight at her, so it knocked out my vision.

name: Kimika
message: "Well, your eyesight will recover in a few minutes, so do take care. I'll be taking my leave."

name: Yuki
message: "Wait a second!"
I checked out my desk, but it didn't look like she'd done anything to it.
Why was she searching my desk?

name: Yuki
message: "She called Mamiya Takuji 'the savior.'"
I couldn't believe it had already turned into that kind of situation. She said there were other believers, too.

name: Yuki
message: "I might need to investigate this."

name: Yuki
message: "No one's here."
I went back to my classroom, but no one was there.
I looked and... there were just a few people.
There was hardly anyone doing club activites.

name: Yuki
message: "I don't see any third-years at all."
It looked like Kagami and Tsukasa's kendo club wasn't meeting today.
I wanted to talk to Yokoyama Yasuko, too.
Huh? I think I just saw someone.
Where did they go?

name: Yuki
message: "Ayana-san? What are you doing?"

name: Ayana
message: "The same as always."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm... Hey, don't you think it's kind of deserted around here? I'd understand if it was Saturday, but there should be more people around after school on a Thursday."

name: Ayana
message: "Really?"

name: Yuki
message: "There are a few people doing stuff with their clubs, but it's like everyone just up and disappeared."

name: Ayana
message: "Then maybe they ran away."

name: Yuki
message: "Ran away?"

name: Ayana
message: "Yes. Maybe they ran off somewhere."
No one here, huh?
I thought Ayana-san might still be hanging around.

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm... I didn't really find out anything new."
Oh well, time to head home.
There really wasn't anyone in the classroom.
I had no choice but to head home.
I didn't find out anything important, though I was almost stabbed by some girl from the next class over.
Well, I guess you could call that important in its own way.
Since I found out that Mamiya Takuji already had followers like that.

name: Yuki
message: "Ugh. This is getting kind of dangerous."
I got the feeling I shouldn't stick my nose in.
This could turn into quite an incident, couldn't it?
But for some reason...
I just couldn't ignore the things that happened. Shiroyama Tsubasa's accident, Takashima Zakuro's suicide, Mamiya Takuji's speech, and the supposed end of the world on July 20th.
I just couldn't treat them as someone else's problem.
I had a feeling they were related to me in some way.
Though I didn't really know why.
If I had to say...

name: Yuki
message: "My ghost is whispering to me."
Something like that?
As I was walking out the school gates, I noticed something strange.
I thought I heard a bunch of people in the courtyard.

name: Yuki
message: "Is that... a person?"
Why were there suspicious people gathering behind the school at this time of day?

name: Yuki
message: "Club activities should be over by now, too. There shouldn't be anyone here anymore, so why are there people back there?"
I chased after them.

name: Yuki
message: "No one's here."

name: Yuki
message: "I'm sure I saw someone running this way."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?!"
An unsettling noise echoed in the air.
The sound of something crumpling and shattering in an instant.
Right in front of me.
I heard that sound, right at my feet.
Something warm was clinging to my ankle.
An incredibly unpleasant... warmth.

name: Yuki
message: "Eek!"
"It hurts!"
"Ihh, ihh hurttss. It..."
"Ihh, ihh, ahhhck..."
Her lungs had probably ruptured. The person who grabbed my leg was wheezing in a way that I could hardly identify as human breathing.
"Ihh hurts... Iiihhhh, uhha..."
My eyes met with the person who was grabbing my ankle.

name: Yuki
message: "M-Mrs. Senagawa?!"
Her face was convulsing.
Her clothes were stained dark red.
It took me a few seconds to realize that it was my teacher.

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What happened?"

name: Senagawa
message: "Ma-Mami... Iiihh."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What?"
She tried desperately to say something.

name: Senagawa
message: "Taku... ji... kun."

name: Yuki
message: "Takuji-kun?"
The light suddenly disappeared from her eyes.
Her pupils dilated.

name: Yuki
message: "Mrs. Senagawa!"
I quickly evaluated the situation.
Other than Mrs. Senagawa's body, there were several small objects lying around that were probably hers.
Among them was her cell phone.
I grabbed the cell phone and started to dial for an ambulance.

name: Yuki
message: "H-Hello, is this the hospital? Could you send an ambulance to Kita High in Suginomiya? Someone fell from the roof."

name: Yuki
message: "Yes, I am. My name is Minakami Yuki. Yes, I'm one of the students here."

name: Yuki
message: "The person who fell is a teacher. Yes, understood."

name: Yuki
message: "Now I need to go tell the people in the staff room."
"I guess there's a reason he's the savior. The time was exact, down to the very minute."

name: Yuki
message: "Th-That voice!"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"
Tachibana-san wrenched the phone from my hand.

name: Kimika
message: "I'm terribly sorry about that, but it just so happens that I need this."

name: Yuki
message: "Y-You again!"

name: Kimika
message: "Jeez, what's the savior thinking? He's so stupid sometimes, letting someone like you get in his way."

name: Yuki
message: "I'm getting in his way? What are you talking about?"

name: Kimika
message: "You wouldn't understand if I told you. Anyway, you're an outsider, so be a good little girl and stay quiet while I wrap this up."

name: Kimika
message: "Oh, I see. So she's been registered since way back then."

name: Kimika
message: "I see. Ms. Kiyokawa referred her."

name: Kimika
message: "Wow, she has a lot of emails."

name: Yuki
message: "Tachibana-san, you..."

name: Kimika
message: "Oh, Minakama-san. Shouldn't you be more worried about her?"

name: Yuki
message: "Ah!"
She was right. I had to head to the staff room.

name: Yuki
message: "Jeez!"
I ran to the staff room.

name: Yuki
message: "Haa, haa, haa, haa, haa, haa, haa, haa."
My head was aching terribly. It felt like I was about to lose consciousness.
Probably because of the mental shock. Or maybe because of something else.

name: Yuki
message: "Ugh!"
My head hurt. Maybe because I started running so suddenly.
My memory from that time is vague.
When I looked up, it was completely dark outside.
My head was spinning so much that I could hardly remember the past few hours. I'm pretty sure I ran around a bunch, and...
I told the teachers what happened, and then I ran back to the accident and waited for the ambulance.

name: Yuki
message: "Haa... That said..."
This headache is awful...

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? There aren't any lights on in Kagami or Tsukasa's rooms. Maybe they're out somewhere."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, it's the last summer they have left to enjoy. I think they've taken up jogging as of late."
The two of them probably weren't aware of what had happened.
I'd been skipping a lot lately, so maybe it was better that I didn't run into them.
Haa...
I turned on my computer.

name: Yuki
message: "I still haven't checked all the threads."
First, I opened my email.

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? What's this?"
Death.

name: Yuki
message: "This title is a bit creepy."
I clicked on that creepy message and opened it.
It read...
another one will die
from the roof into the courtyard
they will keep dying and it can't be stopped
soon it will all end

name: Yuki
message: "This is..."
It was the same message that Yasuko got.
The email address read "takasimazakuro." There was no doubt it.
It was the newest version of the curse mail.
But why?

name: Yuki
message: "I never got this message before, but now I'm getting it too."
Why?
I never got it before.

name: Yuki
message: "Didn't they say this message was showing up on people's phones? Why did my computer get one?"
To be sure, I checked my main address.
I hadn't given this one to any of my friends.

name: Yuki
message: "I was right..."
My main address didn't get one of the messages. The message only showed up on my throwaway account.
In other words, the curse mail only got sent to the free mail account I gave to Yokoyama Kiyoshi.

name: Kiyoshi
message: "But still, everyone browses that site with their cell phones. I don't think I've ever seen anyone go there from a computer."
The curse mail usually went to people's cell phones. And the people who registered on that page usually used their cellphone's email address.

name: Yuki
message: "Which means..."
There was only one reason.

name: Yuki
message: "That curse mail is being sent to everyone who registered on the underground message board."
And that meant...
This had nothing to do with Takashima Zakuro's ghost.
The messages were just being sent out by the administrator of this message board.
In other words, this "supernatural phenomenon" was being orchestrated by a human.
Um...
The top level domain was .net. And next to it was the word "sawayaka."
I might be able to get some information out of this.
I tried searching the domain name with a specialized search service.

name: Yuki
message: "Though there's no way I'm going to be able to pin it down that easily."

name: Yuki
message: "Domain handle... nothing. Of course it's not going to be that easy."

name: Yuki
message: "The server location is Fukuoka. That's a pretty common location. It doesn't tell me anything new."

name: Yuki
message: "The date of registration is June 30th, 2010. It's effective for three years from that date."
Of course, I couldn't find any vital information. They weren't going to let me grab their tail quite that easily.

name: Yuki
message: "No good, huh?"
Anyway, I continued what I was doing last night.

name: Yuki
message: "There are still threads I haven't seen, and I want to see how other people reacted."
4: Takashima mail General
Takashima mail...
So that's what they were calling the curse mail they were getting from Takashima-san.

This could be really dangerous, couldn't it?
This is all Takashima's curse.
The morning of the 15th... This post was from several hours before Mamiya Takuji hit the teacher with the flower vase, wasn't it?
Actually, there were students yelling about some message, but I guess that whole thing was predicted by Takashima's email.

name: Yuki
message: "But still..."
I thought her messages were going out to all the people registered on the message board.
But from what I had read, there were two types of messages: ones that go out to everyone registered, and ones that go out to specific people.

At least, it looked like the people posting under the names 
"Akasaka Megu" and "Satoko" got different messages.
I see...

name: Yuki
message: "And then we have..."
1: Who shall be saved and who shall not 3
This thread was on part three.
The first one was made yesterday at around 10, so the threads were going really fast, way faster than usual on a message board like this.

When I got home,

and seal away the curse.

they stopped coming.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this?"
After that, several other people asked Mamiya for help too.
They were all about problems that people thought were from Takashima's curse, and Mamiya solved all of them.
Then, the new message from 9:20 a.m.
another one will die
from the roof into the courtyard
they will keep dying and it can't be stopped
soon it will all end
That message was sent out to the people registered on the board.
It had more credibility, since it was the same as Mamiya's prediction the day before.
Then today was the day of the prediction.
Senagawa Yui fell from Building C and died.
At this point, no one on the board doubted Mamiya.
He used the special environment that is the Internet to appear charismatic.
The rest was pretty awful.
They took those far-fetched delusions as truth, and it became their reality.

name: Yuki
message: "This is just like..."
Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.
And all the devils besought him, saying, send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.

And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were choked in the sea.
And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.
